content.text:
## Task: Fix [high] Documentation: README.md still documents removed 'Search button' and omits new instant/clear/spinner search behavio
## Step: write-tests

### README.md
```markdown
# File Browser

A full-stack file browser with a .NET Web API backend and a TypeScript SPA frontend.

## Prerequisites

- [.NET 10 SDK](https://dotnet.microsoft.com/download/dotnet/10.0)
- [Node.js v20+](https://nodejs.org/)
- npm (ships with Node.js)

## Quick Start

Run the following commands in order from the repository root.

```bash
# 1. Install JavaScript dependencies
npm install

# 2. Build the frontend
#    Compiles TypeScript in src/ to wwwroot/dist/ and copies src/app.css to wwwroot/dist/app.css
npm run build

# 3. Start the backend server
dotnet run --project TestProject.csproj
```

Open the HTTPS URL printed in the console (e.g. `https://localhost:7146`). Click **Browse Files** to open the file-browser dialog.

## Configuration

The server's home directory (where files are stored) is configurable via the `FileService:HomeDirectory` setting.

- **appsettings.json**: set `FileService.HomeDirectory` (default is `"Home"`).
- **Environment variable**: set `FileService__HomeDirectory` (double underscore).

Relative paths resolve under the app content root. The folder is created automatically on first use (lazily, the first time a file-system operation targets it), so a misconfigured path surfaces on the first request rather than at startup.

## Limits

- **Search results are capped at 500.** Search stops collecting matches once it reaches 500 results, so a broad query may return fewer entries than actually exist. Narrow the query or scope it to a subfolder to see additional matches.
- **Directory copy is limited to 32 levels of nesting.** Copying a tree nested more than 32 levels deep aborts with an IOException whose message is `Directory copy depth limit exceeded (possible cycle)`, to guard against stack overflow on pathological or self-referential directory trees.

## File name handling

Uploaded file names are sanitized before they touch disk, and the rules are enforced identically on every platform so an upload that works on Linux is also valid on Windows:

- **Directory components are stripped.** A submitted name like `../x` or `..\x` is reduced to its final segment (`x`), so an upload can never traverse out of its target directory. Both `/` and `\` are treated as separators.
- **Windows-incompatible names are rejected** with a `400 Bad Request`: names containing characters invalid on Windows (`< > : " | ? * \ /`) or matching a reserved device name (`CON`, `PRN`, `AUX`, `NUL`, `COM1`–`COM9`, `LPT1`–`LPT9`, with or without an extension).

## Symlinks and junctions

Search and copy never follow symbolic links (Linux/macOS) or directory junctions (Windows). A link is still listed by name in browse and search results, but its contents are never scanned or copied, so a link that targets a location outside the home directory cannot leak external files into a search or copy.

## Deep Linking

The app keeps its current view and location in the URL hash, so any folder listing or search result can be bookmarked or shared as a direct link.

- `#/browse` — root folder listing.
- `#/browse/Documents/Reports` — a nested folder.
- `#/search?q=budget&path=Documents` — a search scoped to a folder.

Each path segment is percent-encoded (e.g. spaces become `%20`).

## Running Tests

```bash
# Frontend tests (Vitest)
npm test

# Backend tests (xUnit)
dotnet test TestProject.Tests/TestProject.Tests.csproj
```

## Final End-to-End Verification Checklist

1. **`dotnet build TestProject.csproj`** — project compiles without errors.
2. **`npm test`** — all frontend tests pass.
3. **`npm run build`** — succeeds and produces `wwwroot/dist/app.js` and `wwwroot/dist/app.css`.
4. **`dotnet run`** — serves `index.html` at `/` (visit `https://localhost:7146`).
5. **`GET /api/files/browse?path=`** — returns JSON with `entries`, `folderCount`, `fileCount`, and `totalSize`.
6. **UI dialog** — opens when "Browse Files" is clicked.
7. **UI lists files/folders** — entries render in the table with Name, Size, Modified, and Actions columns.
8. **Upload** — file upload works via the Upload button.
9. **Download** — clicking the Download link retrieves the file.
10. **Delete** — Delete action removes files/folders after confirmation.
11. **Move** — Move action renames/relocates entries via a prompt.
12. **Copy** — Copy action duplicates entries via a prompt.
13. **Search** — Search input and button filter entries recursively by name.

```
You are a fix agent. Resolve the following final-review finding.

Review dimension: Documentation
Severity: high
File: README.md:88
Title: README.md still documents removed 'Search button' and omits new instant/clear/spinner search behavior

Finding:
The fixer's diff only modified dev-diary.md; README.md was not touched at all. The branch replaced the standalone 'Search' <button> with an instant, debounced (~200 ms) search-as-you-type input that supports Enter (commit immediately), Escape (clear the query and return to browse), an in-input clear (✕) button toggled purely via CSS :placeholder-shown (no JS), and a spinner shown in the results area while a search fetch is in flight. README.md still misleads in two ways: (1) the 'Final End-to-End Verification Checklist' item 13 still reads 'Search — Search input and button filter entries recursively by name.' — a QA/reviewer following the checklist will look for a Search button that no longer exists; (2) the README has detailed behavior sections (Limits, File name handling, Symlinks, Deep Linking) but nothing describing the search UX, so users reading the docs won't discover the instant/clear/spinner/Escape behaviors. The README must be corrected so its checklist matches the current UI and a concise section documents the new search experience.

Fix instructions (follow exactly; make targeted, minimal changes):
Edit README.md in two places to make it match the current search UX.

1) Update the checklist. In README.md, change the line numbered 13 under '## Final End-to-End Verification Checklist' from:

13. **Search** — Search input and button filter entries recursively by name.

to:

13. **Search** — The search input filters entries recursively by name with instant, debounced (200 ms) search-as-you-type; pressing Enter submits immediately, pressing Escape clears the query and returns to browse, the in-input ✕ clear button resets the query, and a spinner shows in the results area while a search fetch is in flight.

2) Add a new behavior section. Insert a new '## Search' section immediately BEFORE the existing '## Deep Linking' section (i.e. right after the '## Symlinks and junctions' section) with the following content (preserve the existing section ordering otherwise):

## Search

Search is instant and debounced. As you type in the search box, a query fires ~200 ms after you stop typing — no separate Search button to click. Enter submits the current query immediately. Escape clears the query and returns to browse. When the input has text, a clear (✕) button appears inside it; click it (or clear the input) to reset the query. While a search request is in flight, a spinner is shown in the results area.

Do not modify any other section. Verify the markdown still renders (matching heading levels, blank lines around the new section).

# File Browser

A full-stack file browser with a .NET Web API backend and a TypeScript SPA frontend.

## Prerequisites

- [.NET 10 SDK](https://dotnet.microsoft.com/download/dotnet/10.0)

- npm (ships with Node.js)

## Quick Start

Run the following commands in order from the repository root.

```bash

npm install

# 2. Build the frontend
#    Compiles TypeScript in src/ to wwwroot/dist/ and copies src/app.css to wwwroot/dist/app.css
npm run build

# 3. Start the backend server
dotnet run --project TestProject.csproj

Open the HTTPS URL printed in the console (e.g. `https://localhost:7146`). Click **Browse Files** to open the file-browser dialog.

## Configuration

- **Directory copy is limited to 32 levels of nesting.** Copying a tree nested more than 32 levels deep aborts with an IOException whose message is `Directory copy depth limit exceeded (possible cycle)`, to guard against stack overflow on pathological or self-referential directory trees.

## File name handling

Uploaded file names are sanitized before they touch disk, and the rules are enforced identically on every platform so an upload that works on Linux is also valid on Windows:

- **Directory components are stripped.** A submitted name like `../x` or `..\x` is reduced to its final segment (`x`), so an upload can never traverse out of its target directory. Both `/` and `\` are treated as separators.
- **Windows-incompatible names are rejected** with a `400 Bad Request`: names containing characters invalid on Windows (`< > : " | ? * \ /`) or matching a reserved device name (`CON`, `PRN`, `AUX`, `NUL`, `COM1`–`COM9`, `LPT1`–`LPT9`, with or without an extension).

Search and copy never follow symbolic links (Linux/macOS) or directory junctions (Windows). The app keeps its current view and location in the URL hash, so any folder listing or search result can be bookmarked or shared as a direct link.

Each path segment is percent-encoded (e.g. spaces become `%20`).

## Running Tests

```bash
# Frontend tests (Vitest)
npm test

# Backend tests (xUnit)
dotnet test TestProject.Tests/TestProject.Tests.csproj
```

## Final End-to-End Verification Checklist

1. **`dotnet build TestProject.csproj`** — project compiles without errors.
2. **`npm test`** — all frontend tests pass.
3. **`npm run build`** — succeeds and produces `wwwroot/dist/app.js` and `wwwroot/dist/app.css`.
4. **`dotnet run`** — serves `index.html` at `/` (visit `https://localhost:7146`).

6. **UI dialog** — opens when "Browse Files" is clicked.
7. **UI lists files/folders** — entries render in the table with Name, Size, Modified, and Actions columns.

4. Assert the section ordering: Symlinks and junctions → Search → Deep Linking

Let me be careful about the assertions. I don't want to be overly brittle (e.g., requiring exact string matches), but I do want to verify the key facts.

Let me now design the test file.

For reading README.md, I'll use:
```ts
import { readFileSync } from 'node:fs';
import { resolve, dirname } from 'node:path';
import { fileURLToPath } from 'node:url';

const __dirname = dirname(fileURLToPath(import.meta.url));
const README_PATH = resolve(__dirname, '../README.md');
const README = readFileSync(README_PATH, 'utf8');
```

Since the test is at `src/readme.test.ts`, `../README.md` resolves to repo-root/README.md. Good.

I'll pin the environment to `node` since these are static-asset tests that only read a file.

Now let me design helper functions to extract sections and the checklist item.

**Extracting a markdown section:** A section starts with `## Title` and ends at the next `## ` heading (or EOF). I can use a regex like:
```ts
function getSection(md: string, title: string): string | null {
  // Match a level-2 heading with the given title, capturing until the next ## or EOF
  const re = new RegExp(`^## ${escapeRegex(title)}\\s*$(^[]*)(?=^## |$)`, 'm');
  ...
}
```

Actually, parsing markdown sections with regex can be tricky. Let me use a simpler approach: split the document by `## ` headings.

Let me write a helper:
```ts
interface Section { title: string; body: string; start: number; }

function parseH2Sections(md: string): Section[] {
  const sections: Section[] = [];
  const re = /^##[ \t]+(.+?)\s*$/gm;
  let match;
  const headers: { title: string; index: number }[] = [];
  while ((match = re.exec(md)) !== null) {
    headers.push({ title: match[1].trim(), index: match.index });
  }
  for (let i = 0; i < headers.length; i++) {
    const start = headers[i].index;
    const headerLen = md.slice(start).indexOf('\n') + 1;
    const bodyStart = start + headerLen;
    const bodyEnd = i + 1 < headers.length ? headers[i + 1].index : md.length;
    const body = md.slice(bodyStart, bodyEnd);
    sections.push({ title: headers[i].title, body, start });
  }
  return sections;
}
```

This gives me sections with their bodies and start indices, so I can check ordering.

Then for each section I need, I can find it by title.

For the checklist item 13, I need to extract the content of line starting with `13.`. Let me use a regex:
```ts
function getChecklistItem(md: string, n: number): string | null {
  const re = new RegExp(`^${n}\\.\\s+(.+)$`, 'm');
  const m = md.match(re);
  return m ? m[1] : null;
}
```

```ts
function expectAllPresent(label: string, text: string, substrings: string[]) {
  const missing = substrings.filter((s) => !text.includes(s));
  expect(missing, `${label}: missing ${JSON.stringify(missing)}`).toEqual([]);
}
```

function expectNonePresent(label: string, text: string, substrings: string[]) {
  const present = substrings.filter((s) => text.includes(s));
  expect(present, `${label}: should not contain ${JSON.stringify(present)}`).toEqual([]);
}
```

Let me write the helper for parsing sections carefully. Edge: the body extraction. Let me just capture the raw substring from the heading line to the next heading.

```ts
interface Section {
  title: string;
  headingLine: string;
  body: string;
  start: number;
}

function parseH2Sections(md: string): Section[] {
  const sections: Section[] = [];
  const headingRe = /^##(?!#)[ \t]+(.+?)\r?\n/gm;
  const headers: { title: string; start: number; lineEnd: number }[] = [];
  let m: RegExpExecArray | null;
  while ((m = headingRe.exec(md)) !== null) {
    headers.push({
      title: m[1].trim(),
      start: m.index,
      lineEnd: m.index + m[0].length,
    });
  }
  for (let i = 0; i < headers.length; i++) {
    const h = headers[i];
    const bodyEnd = i + 1 < headers.length ? headers[i + 1].start : md.length;
    sections.push({
      title: h.title,
      headingLine: md.slice(h.start, h.lineEnd),
      body: md.slice(h.lineEnd, bodyEnd),
      start: h.start,
    });
  }
  return sections;
}
```

This looks good.

For getting the ordered titles: `sections.map(s => s.title)`.

For finding a section: `sections.find(s => s.title === title)`.

## Summary

I wrote a documentation test (`src/readme.test.ts`) that pins README.md to the actual search UX, following the project's established static-asset doc-test pattern (`src/index.html.test.ts`). The tests read README.md from disk and assert:
1. **Checklist item 13** no longer references the removed "Search button" and instead documents the instant/debounced (200 ms) search-as-you-type, Enter (immediate), Escape (clears + returns to browse), in-input ✕ clear button, and spinner.
2. A new **`## Search`** section exists (exactly one, level-2 heading) and documents those same behaviors.
3. **Section ordering** is preserved: `Symlinks and junctions` → `Search` → `Deep Linking`.

These tests will turn green once the apply-fix step edits README.md per the fix instructions.
